title: Conservative Client Fixed Income Rule
content: This document establishes mandatory suitability guidelines for recommending fixed income securities to clients classified as conservative risk profile investors. All associates and above must adhere to these requirements when constructing portfolios or making investment recommendations for conservative clients, in accordance with FINRA Rule 2111(a) reasonable-basis, customer-specific, and quantitative suitability obligations. Conservative clients are defined as those with risk tolerance scores of 1-3 on our firm's risk assessment scale, typically including retirees, pre-retirees within 5 years of retirement, or clients with stated capital preservation objectives. For these clients, fixed income allocations should comprise 60-85% of total investable assets, with the remainder in low-volatility equity strategies. Approved fixed income instruments for conservative clients include: US Treasury securities with maturities up to 10 years, investment-grade corporate bonds rated BBB- or higher by S&P, municipal bonds rated A- or higher for tax-sensitive accounts, and certificates of deposit from FDIC-insured institutions. The maximum single issuer concentration for corporate bonds shall not exceed 5% of the fixed income allocation, excluding US Treasury securities. Duration risk must be actively managed with portfolio-level modified duration not exceeding 6.0 years under normal market conditions. Associates must verify that recommended bonds have minimum issue sizes of $250 million to ensure adequate liquidity. High-yield bonds, convertible securities, emerging market debt, and unrated municipal bonds are strictly prohibited for conservative clients unless specifically approved by a Senior Advisor or above through our enhanced due diligence process. When constructing laddered bond portfolios, ensure maturity distribution does not concentrate more than 25% of holdings in any single calendar year. For bond mutual funds and ETFs, expense ratios should not exceed 0.75% for active strategies or 0.25% for passive index funds. Credit quality requirements mandate minimum weighted average credit rating of A- for any diversified fixed income fund recommendation. Associates must document the suitability rationale in client files, including specific reference to the client's investment objectives, time horizon, liquidity needs, and risk tolerance as gathered through our Know Your Customer process. Yield chasing is explicitly prohibited - recommendations must be based on total return potential and alignment with client goals rather than current yield metrics alone. Interest rate sensitivity discussions are mandatory when recommending individual bonds or bond funds with duration exceeding 4 years. For municipal bond recommendations, associates must verify the client's tax bracket justifies the after-tax equivalent yield advantage and confirm state-specific tax benefits where applicable. All fixed income recommendations require secondary review by the client's designated Advisor before implementation. Monthly portfolio reviews must confirm continued alignment with these guidelines, with any deviations requiring immediate corrective action or formal exception approval. This rule supersedes previous versions dated prior to 2025 and reflects current regulatory guidance and firm risk management policies. Questions regarding interpretation or exception requests should be directed to the Fixed Income Committee through normal channels.
